title: Research P2P Streaming Implementation Options
description: Research P2P streaming implementation options for PeerTube iOS client. Investigate WebTorrent integration possibilities, battery/bandwidth implications, and implementation complexity. This is marked as optional for v0.1 but needs investigation for future versions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Research document on P2P streaming options
- WebTorrent Swift library analysis
- Battery and bandwidth impact assessment
- Implementation complexity evaluation
- Recommendation for v0.1 vs future versions

title: Design Local Subscription Data Model
description: Design and implement Core Data model for storing local channel subscriptions. Create entities for subscriptions, channels, and cached video metadata without requiring user authentication.
acceptance_criteria:
- Core Data model for local subscriptions
- LocalSubscription entity with channel info
- Cached channel and video metadata entities
- Data persistence and migration setup
- Repository pattern for data access

title: Implement Core API Service Layer
description: Implement core API endpoints needed for v0.1 features: video details, video lists, channel details, channel videos, and search functionality. Create service layer methods that use the API client.
acceptance_criteria:
- Video service (getVideo, listVideos, searchVideos)
- Channel service (getChannel, getChannelVideos, searchChannels)  
- Instance service (getConfig, getAbout)
- Proper async/await implementation
- Integration tests with real PeerTube instance
- Service layer documentation

title: Implement Subscription Management
description: Implement subscription management functionality. Allow users to subscribe/unsubscribe to channels, manage their subscription list, and handle cross-instance channel subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Subscribe/unsubscribe functionality
- Subscription list management UI
- Cross-instance channel support (@channel@instance.com format)
- Import/export subscription lists (OPML or JSON)
- Subscription synchronization logic

title: Create Xcode iOS Project
description: Create a new iOS project in Xcode with SwiftUI framework. Configure basic project settings, bundle identifier, deployment targets, and initial app structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- Xcode project created with SwiftUI
- iOS deployment target set to 16.0+
- Basic app structure with main ContentView
- Project builds and runs successfully
- Bundle identifier configured

title: Build PeerTube API Client
description: Implement the networking layer for communicating with PeerTube instances. Create API client with support for different instance URLs, request/response handling, error handling, and rate limiting.
acceptance_criteria:
- APIClient class with configurable instance URL
- HTTP request/response handling with URLSession
- Proper error handling and status code processing
- Rate limiting implementation (50 requests per 10 seconds)
- Support for both authenticated and anonymous requests
- Unit tests for API client functionality

title: Analyze Comment System Implementation
description: Analyze comment system implementation options for PeerTube videos. Research the PeerTube comments API, threading structure, moderation features, and UI/UX considerations for mobile comment display.
acceptance_criteria:
- PeerTube comments API analysis
- Comment threading and reply structure research
- Mobile comment UI/UX best practices
- Moderation and filtering options
- Implementation complexity assessment
- Recommendation for inclusion in v0.1 vs later versions

title: Create PeerTube API Swift Models
description: Create Swift models based on PeerTube API responses. Generate or manually create models for Video, Channel, Account, and other core API objects. Include proper JSON decoding with Codable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Swift models for PeerTube API objects (Video, Channel, Account, etc.)
- JSON decoding implementation with Codable
- Model relationships properly defined
- Unit tests for model decoding
- Documentation of model properties
close_reason: Completed all acceptance criteria: Created comprehensive Swift models for PeerTube API objects (Actor, Account, VideoChannel, Video, VideoDetails, Instance), implemented JSON decoding with Codable, defined proper model relationships with summary variants, created extensive unit tests, and provided detailed documentation.

title: Create Video Detail Page UI
description: Create the video detail page showing video metadata, description, channel information, and embedded video player. Include subscribe button, share functionality, and related video suggestions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Video detail SwiftUI view
- Video title, description, and metadata display
- Channel information with subscribe button
- Video player integration
- Share functionality
- Related videos section (optional)

title: Build Subscription Feed Aggregation
description: Create subscription feed aggregation logic that fetches latest videos from all subscribed channels and displays them chronologically. Implement background refresh and caching strategy.
acceptance_criteria:
- Feed aggregation from multiple channels
- Chronological video sorting by publication date
- Background refresh capability
- Efficient API usage with rate limiting
- Pull-to-refresh functionality
- Offline cached content display

title: Implement Video Quality Selection
description: Add video quality selection functionality. Allow users to manually select video quality from available formats, and implement automatic quality selection based on network conditions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manual quality selection UI
- Automatic quality based on network speed
- Quality options parsed from PeerTube video files
- Seamless quality switching during playback
- User preference persistence for quality settings

title: Setup Core Dependencies and Architecture
description: Set up core dependencies and architecture patterns. Configure package manager, Core Data for local storage, networking foundation, and dependency injection patterns.
acceptance_criteria:
- Swift Package Manager configured
- Core Data stack setup for local storage
- URLSession networking foundation
- Basic app architecture documented
- Core dependency injection container setup
close_reason: Completed all acceptance criteria: Swift Package Manager configured, Core Data stack implemented, URLSession networking foundation created, architecture documented, and dependency injection container setup complete.

title: Design Subscription Feed UI
description: Create the subscription feed page showing chronologically ordered videos from all subscribed channels. Include pull-to-refresh, infinite scroll, and video thumbnails with metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- Subscription feed SwiftUI view
- Chronological video list from subscriptions
- Pull-to-refresh functionality
- Infinite scroll/pagination
- Video thumbnails and metadata
- Empty state when no subscriptions
- Loading states and error handling

title: Implement App Settings and Configuration
description: Add app settings and configuration options including default PeerTube instance, video quality preferences, subscription refresh intervals, and privacy settings.
acceptance_criteria:
- Settings screen with SwiftUI
- Default instance configuration
- Video playback preferences
- Subscription refresh settings
- Privacy and data usage options
- Settings persistence with UserDefaults

title: Comprehensive Testing Implementation
description: Create comprehensive unit and integration tests for the PeerTube API client, local subscription system, and video player components. Ensure robust error handling and edge case coverage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Unit tests for API models and client
- Integration tests with live PeerTube instances
- Subscription management tests
- Video player functionality tests
- Error handling and edge case tests
- CI/CD pipeline setup for automated testing

title: Research Authentication and User Account Integration
description: Research authentication and user account integration options for future versions. While v0.1 focuses on local subscriptions, analyze PeerTube's OAuth flow, user account features, and how to migrate from local-only to authenticated user experience.
acceptance_criteria:
- PeerTube OAuth/authentication flow analysis
- User account feature comparison (local vs authenticated)
- Migration path from local subscriptions to authenticated accounts
- Privacy implications and user choice options
- Implementation roadmap for future authentication support

title: Video Playback and Streaming
description: Implement video playback functionality including streaming, player controls, quality selection, and Picture-in-Picture support. This epic covers the core video viewing experience using AVKit/AVPlayer with HLS streaming support.

title: PeerTube iOS Client - Project Setup and Architecture
description: Set up the foundational iOS project structure, dependencies, and core architecture for the PeerTube Swift client. This includes creating the Xcode project, setting up the SwiftUI app structure, and establishing the basic navigation and data models.

title: Add Picture-in-Picture Support
description: Enable Picture-in-Picture (PiP) support for video playback. Configure AVPlayer to support PiP mode and handle the appropriate iOS lifecycle events.
acceptance_criteria:
- PiP functionality enabled in AVPlayer
- Proper app lifecycle handling for PiP mode
- PiP controls working correctly
- Background audio session configuration
- Testing on device (PiP not supported in simulator)

title: PeerTube API Integration
description: Build the PeerTube API client with Swift models and networking layer. Includes authentication handling, API request/response processing, and integration with various PeerTube instances across the federation.

title: Build Channel Page UI
description: Build the channel page displaying channel information, avatar/banner, description, and list of channel videos. Include subscription status and video filtering/sorting options.
acceptance_criteria:
- Channel profile SwiftUI view
- Channel avatar, banner, and description
- Channel video list with pagination
- Subscribe/unsubscribe functionality
- Video sorting options (newest, oldest, popular)
- Channel statistics display

title: User Interface and Navigation
description: Create user interface screens for video details, channel pages, and subscription feed. Includes SwiftUI views, navigation, and user interaction handling for the core app experience.

title: Local Subscriptions System
description: Implement local-only subscriptions system that stores channel subscriptions on device without requiring user login. Includes subscription management, feed aggregation, and chronological video display from subscribed channels.

title: Implement Basic App Navigation Structure
description: Create the basic navigation structure using SwiftUI TabView or NavigationStack. Set up main app tabs for Subscriptions, Browse/Discover, and Settings.
acceptance_criteria:
- Main TabView with core app sections
- Navigation between different screens
- Tab bar configured with appropriate icons
- Navigation state management setup

title: Implement Basic AVKit Video Player
description: Implement basic video player using AVKit and AVPlayer. Create video player view that can play videos from HLS streaming URLs or direct file URLs from PeerTube.
acceptance_criteria:
- AVPlayerViewController integration with SwiftUI
- Video playback from HLS and direct file URLs
- Basic player controls (play, pause, seek)
- Video player view component
- Error handling for unsupported formats
